title: DEFLECTION DRUG TREATMENT PROGRAM - 211info
text: DESCHUTES COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE DEFLECTION DRUG TREATMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps people quickly access drug treatment. People can ask for help even if they are not in legal trouble. Police officers can sometimes take individuals directly to treatment instead of jail. The program works with the court, Parole and Probation, the District Attorney’s Office, and defense lawyers to support people who need treatment. Participants are paired with a peer navigator and receive case management/wraparound services. Services may include help with obtaining birth certificates and IDs, building resumes, finding jobs, housing and transportation support, signing up for health insurance, medication-assisted treatment, detox services, coordination with ODHS, and referrals to other resources. 63333 W Highway 20 Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6:30pm Email (541) 585-0008 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 585-0008 Taylor Center Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for more information. Call or walk in to Taylor Center: 358 NE Marshall Avenue, Bend OR 97701. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Deschutes County Services: Case/Care Management * Substance Use Disorder Issues Substance Use Disorder Referrals Other Services Offered At This Location: CRIMINAL CORRECTIONAL SYSTEM - JAIL INFORMATION INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CENTRAL OREGON PARTNERSHIPS FOR YOUTH LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES - SHERIFF'S OFFICE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. DESCHUTES COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE
